slides.title: ASP.NET Core Architecture
slides.subtitle:
Mastering High-Performance .NET
From Middleware Pipelines to Cloud Native Microservices

slides.name: Aravind Govindhasamy
slides.title: Software Developer
slides.experience: Specialist in .NET, C#, and Distributed Systems
slides.oss_experience: Open Source Contributor & Technical Content Creator

list.text: Speed: Optimized for high-throughput and low-latency.
Cross-Platform: Runs identical everywhere (Windows, Linux, macOS).

box.title: A cross-platform, high-performance web server for ASP.NET Core.
Consolidated Startup and Program logic into a unified entry point using Top-Level Statements.

list.text: IHost: The core abstraction for managing application lifecycle.
Universal: Same pattern for APIs, Web Apps, and Background Services.

box.title: Encapsulates resources like Dependency Injection, Logging, and Configuration for web and worker services.

list.text: Chain of Responsibility: Each component chooses whether to pass the request to the next component.
Bidirectional: Handles both the incoming request and outgoing response.

box.title: Software that's assembled into an app pipeline to handle requests and responses.
Ordering is critical! Typical flow: Error Handling -> HSTS -> HTTPS Redirection -> Static Files -> Routing -> Auth -> Endpoints.
Extend the pipeline logic with custom classes or inline delegates.
Registration of services is done in the WebApplicationBuilder using the Services property.

list.text: Transient: Created every time they are requested.
Scoped: Created once per client request (connection).
Singleton: Created once and shared across the lifetime of the application.

box.title: ASP.NET Core supports three primary lifetimes for managed services.
The standard way to receive dependencies in controllers and other services.

list.text: Resolution: Automatically handles nested dependencies.
Disposal: Automatically disposes IDisposable services when their lifetime ends.

box.title: The container responsible for resolving services at runtime based on their registered lifetime.

list.text: ORM: Map POCO (Plain Old CLR Object) classes to database tables.
Providers: SQL Server, PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, etc.

box.title: A lightweight, extensible, and cross-platform version of the popular Entity Framework data access technology.
The central class for interacting with the database using EF Core.
Write type-safe queries directly in C# that are converted to SQL by EF Core.

box.code:
# shell
dotnet ef migrations add InitialCreate
dotnet ef database update
box.title: Evolve your database schema over time without losing data by using migration scripts.

list.text: Annotations: Simple mapping like [Key] or [Required].
Fluent API: Advanced configuration using modelBuilder.Entity().Property(p => p.X)...

box.title: Two ways to configure model mapping: using attributes on classes or the ModelBuilder at the DbContext level.
Traditional approach using classes and attributes for routing and action results.
A high-performance, lightweight approach to building APIs in .NET 6+ with minimal code.

box.code: public record UserDto([Required] string Name, [EmailAddress] string Email);
box.title: Automatically map request data to C# objects and validate using Data Annotations or FluentValidation.

list.text: Attribute Routing: Directly on controller methods.
Swagger: Automatically generated documentation for your API.

box.title: Define complex routes with constraints and use OpenAPI/Swagger for discovery.

list.text: Ok(): Returns 200 OK.
NotFound(): Returns 404.

box.title: Return various data types (JSON, XML, File) and status codes using built-in helper methods.

list.text: UserManager: Manage users and their properties.
SignInManager: Handle login/logout flows.

box.title: A comprehensive membership system that handles users, passwords, profile data, roles, and more.
Stateless authentication for APIs using JSON Web Tokens issued by IdentityServer or Microsoft Entra ID.
Define complex authorization requirements that go beyond simple roles.

list.text: Claim: A statement about the user (e.g., Email, DateOfBirth).
Identity: A collection of claims representing the authenticated user.

box.title: User information is stored as key-value pairs (claims) that can be used for granular access control.

list.text: OIDC: Layer on top of OAuth2 for identity.
External Login: 'Login with...' functionality.

box.title: Deep integration with external providers like Google, GitHub, or Microsoft for shared authentication.
A new middleware in .NET 7+ that caches responses on the server to reduce database and CPU load.

list.text: Protobuf: Binary serialization for speed and efficiency.
IDL: Define services and messages in .proto files.

box.title: A high-performance RPC framework for contract-first, cross-platform microservice communication.
Reduce the size of transmitted data using Gzip or Brotli compression middleware.
Protect your application from abuse by limiting the number of requests a client can make.

list.text: No JIT: Immediate execution without cold starts.
Footprint: Significant reduction in memory consumption.

box.title: Publish your application as a native executable for ultra-fast startup and reduced memory usage.
Expose endpoints for orchestration tools (K8s) to monitor application availability.
ASP.NET Core is designed for containers with official lightweight images from Microsoft.

list.text: Flexible: Override config per environment (Dev, Staging, Prod).
Security: Use Key Vault for sensitive data in cloud.

box.title: Support for multiple sources: JSON, Environment Variables, User Secrets, and Azure Key Vault.

list.text: Metrics: Track resource usage and performance.
Tracing: Follow requests across service boundaries.

box.title: Standardized metrics and distributed tracing for monitoring large-scale systems.

list.text: App Service: Fully managed web hosting.
GitHub Actions: Automated CI/CD pipelines to Azure.

box.title: Seamless deployment and integration with Azure App Service, Functions, and Cosmos DB.
The most popular testing framework for .NET, known for its extensibility and parallel execution.

box.code:
var mockRepo = new Mock<ITradeRepository>();
mockRepo.Setup(r => r.GetById(1)).Returns(new Trade());
box.title: Simulate external dependencies like repositories or services for isolated unit testing.
Use WebApplicationFactory to bootstrap your entire application in-memory for end-to-end API testing.
Test your data layer in isolation using an In-Memory database or SQLite in-process.
Automatically run tests whenever code changes occur for rapid feedback loops.

list.text: Unrivaled performance with Kestrel and Native AOT.
Modular, testable, and cloud-ready by design.
Unified ecosystem for Web, Cloud, and Microservices.

box.title: ASP.NET Core is a world-class, high-performance framework for building modern web applications.
Any questions? Thank you!
